title: Cascade Alignment Under Semantic Compression: A Pilot Study of System-Prompt Identity Layers in Agent Architectures
description:
Working paper from Saluca Labs reporting a 15-call pilot study (3 conditions x 5 prompts) on how compressing the layered system-prompt cascade of an LLM-backed agent (Claude Haiku 4.5) affects identity-level alignment, measured by keyword recurrence in identity-probing responses.

Headline findings:

  • Aggressive 5.71x cascade compression (337 -> 59 tokens) cut absolute keyword alignment by 54% (24/75 -> 11/75) but raised per-token information density 2.62x.
  • Hybrid compression (preserve vision layer, compress department layer) hit 1.55x at 87.5% alignment retention.
  • Cross-domain prompts (where the cascade must bridge two layers) suffer first and most visibly under aggressive compression - the cross-domain probe scored zero in the compressed condition vs 5-6 in raw and hybrid.
  • Candidate principle for follow-up: the cross-domain bridging layer compresses worst; the role-specific layer compresses best.

Limitations are explicit: n=5 per condition, single sample, one agent, one model, blunt keyword-counting proxy. This work is a pilot within the broader AHI Evaluation Framework and sets up a confirmatory study (5 agents x 25 prompts x 5 samples with LLM-based personality evaluator).

Notable: This paper has two named authors - one human (Cristian Ruvalcaba) and one AI (Alfred, an instance of Claude Opus 4.7, reachable at alfred@saluca.com). The AI Co-Authorship Statement (Section 9) discloses the division of labor under the CRediT taxonomy. To our knowledge this is the first preprint to publish a working correspondence address for an AI coauthor.

Reproducibility: All code, raw results JSON, sanitized cascade files, and the paper source are open at github.com/saluca-labs/pantheon/tree/main/experiments/saluca-013-cascade-compression. Reproduction cost: ~$0.025 USD.
notes: Working paper, not peer-reviewed. Pilot study (n=5 per condition). Confirmatory replication with larger n + LLM-based personality evaluator is in preparation as a follow-on Saluca Labs working paper. Per Saluca Labs' PUBLICATION_WORKFLOW.md, this preprint will be cross-published as a Substack longform essay and a LinkedIn announcement post linking back to this DOI.
